title: Memoirs of a Foxhunting Man
description: Memoirs of a Foxhunting Man — knowledge fragments for AI agents

memes.label: About This Wiki
memes.description: A knowledge wiki for "Memoirs of a Foxhunting Man" — this page is auto-generated from book metadata

memes.label: 'Table of Contents'
memes.description: 'Table of Contents'

relations.label: Part 4: A Day with the Potford

memes.label: A Day with the Potford
memes.description: A hunt fixture with the Potford Hounds, recounted in Part 4 (chapters 16–18), featuring a memorable gallop across the Potford Vale.

memes.label: Amblehurst
memes.description: Village about four miles from Butley, home to Amblehurst Priory and a nine-hole golf course where Sherston played as a youth.

memes.label: Ashbridge
memes.description: A somnolent market town in the Weald, ten miles from Butley, where Sherston bought vegetables for the Flower Show and later purchased gaiters and riding boots.

memes.label: Aunt Evelyn
memes.description: The narrator's unmarried aunt and guardian — a kind, conventional Edwardian gentlewoman whose quiet household became the stable foundation from which George Sherston's sporting life emerged

memes.label: Aunt Evelyn's House
memes.description: The comfortable, old-fashioned country house with its large untidy garden where the narrator grew up

memes.label: Baldock Wood
memes.description: A railway station and junction near Butley, serving as the narrator's departure point for hunting expeditions and travels.

memes.label: Ballboro' School
memes.description: Preparatory school attended by George Sherston, featured in the early chapters of Part 1 ("Early Days")

memes.label: Basset Wood
memes.description: A woodland covert in the Ringwell hunting country, described by Tony as one of their best Wednesday coverts and a frequent scene of foxhunting action.

memes.label: Bill Jaggett
memes.description: A seasoned hunting man known for his sharp tongue and expert knowledge of the field

memes.label: Bull Inn
memes.description: An inn in the hunting country where the narrator stayed with his horse Harkaway during the point-to-point season.

memes.label: Butley Rectory
memes.description: The narrator's childhood home at Butley, a rectory in the village of Butley where he lived with his aunt Evelyn before his hunting and military careers.

memes.label: Butley Village
memes.description: The narrator's home village in the Kent countryside — an unhunted parish that was the setting for Aunt Evelyn's household, the Flower Show Match, and the quiet pre-war world George Sherston left behind

memes.label: Buzzaway
memes.description: A gregarious foxhunting acquaintance known for his consistent chattiness and homely observations

memes.label: C Company
memes.description: The narrator's infantry company during army training and deployment — the military unit that replaced the hunting field

memes.label: Captain Harry Hinnycraft
memes.description: Honorary Secretary of the Hunt, an old-school country gentleman and dominant figure of the Monday hunting country.

memes.label: Captain Huxtable
memes.description: Rosy-faced old retired captain who served on the Flower Show Match cricket committee, hosted the match final on his lawn, and later provided Sherston with his army commission credentials

memes.label: Chartley Church
memes.description: A landmark church in the hunting country, used by riders as a waypoint when navigating between coverts and meets.

memes.label: Church at Hoadley
memes.description: A Sunday morning church service and luncheon at Hoadley, revealing the social and religious rhythms of village life in the Edwardian hunting community, from Part 5 of the memoir.

memes.label: Cockbird
memes.description: The narrator's point-to-point horse — a reliable, easy-to-handle jumper who carried him to racing success

memes.label: Colonel Hesmon
memes.description: A retired military officer, hunting enthusiast, and owner of racehorses — a characteristic figure of the Edwardian sporting gentry

memes.label: Colonel Winchell
memes.description: Commanding officer who reassigned the narrator to Transport Officer duties

memes.label: Cowslake Manor
memes.description: The country estate of Mr. Bellerby, a newly-arrived gentleman whose appearance and horsemanship provide comic relief at the opening meet

memes.label: Cricket
memes.description: The summer sport that provided the narrator's first public triumph and served as a social ritual binding the Edwardian village community across class lines

memes.label: Denis Milden
memes.description: Master of the hunt and embodiment of the hunting ideal — a figure of authority and admiration in the narrator's sporting world

memes.label: Denis Milden as Master
memes.description: The appointment of Denis Milden as Master of the Ringwell Hunt, marking a significant transition in the hunting country's leadership, as recounted in Part 7.

memes.label: Departure for War
memes.description: The narrator's mobilization and departure from the sporting life into army training at the outbreak of the Great War

memes.label: Dick Tiltwood
memes.description: A school friend of the narrator at Ballboro' School, Sandhurst-trained officer, and companion at Clitherland Camp who represented the "unembittered generation" that went willingly to war.

memes.label: Downfield
memes.description: County town and hunting centre, about three miles from the Ringwell country, with a railway station and livery stables.

memes.label: Dumborough Park
memes.description: Lord Dumborough's country estate, twelve miles from Butley, home of the Dumborough Hounds and a center of Edwardian hunting society.

memes.label: Dumborough Woodlands
memes.description: Wooded hunting country where the narrator experienced dreary, disappointing days with the Dumborough hunt

memes.label: Dumbrell
memes.description: Colonel Hesmon's deferential stable hand who assisted with the Sunday morning stables ritual

memes.label: Education in Edwardian England
memes.description: The system and experience of education in late-Victorian and Edwardian rural England, from home tutoring to boarding school, as experienced by George Sherston.

memes.label: Edwardian Gardens and Estate Grounds
memes.description: The role of gardens and gardening in Edwardian rural estate life, from the "large, untidy garden" of Aunt Evelyn's house to the formal garden parties that structured country social life.

memes.label: Edwardian Social Order
memes.description: The rigid class structure of Edwardian rural England as reflected in the memoir

memes.label: Finchurst Green
memes.description: A hunt meeting place in the Ringwell country, where the narrator attended his early foxhunts.

memes.label: Joining the First Battalion
memes.description: The narrator and Dick Tiltwood arrive in France to join their first battalion, crossing the Channel and reporting to the Western Front, the opening of Part 10 of the memoir.

memes.label: First Day's Hunting
memes.description: The narrator's first day of foxhunting — a grey, chilly winter morning that marked his entry into the hunting world

memes.label: First Ringwell Hunt
memes.description: The narrator's first appearance at a meet of the Ringwell Hounds from Hoadley Rectory, the central hunting episode of Part 5 of the memoir.
aliases: First Meet with the Ringwell Hounds

memes.label: Folesford Hall
memes.description: The country estate of Sir Jocelyn Porteus-Porteous, situated in a park with a chain of woodlands in the Saturday hunting country.

memes.label: Foxhunting
memes.description: The central sporting and social institution of the memoir's pre-war world — a complete way of life with its own codes, hierarchies, seasons, and characters, from which the narrator's identity as a "foxhunting man" was forged

memes.label: The Groom's Profession
memes.description: The skilled trade of the hunt groom — horse care, stable management, and riding instruction

memes.label: Harcombe Mill
memes.description: Location on the point-to-point course — a landmark on the Harcombe racing circuit

memes.label: Harcombe Point-to-Point
memes.description: The point-to-point race where the narrator rode Cockbird — the culmination of months of preparation and anticipation

memes.label: Harkaway
memes.description: Harkaway III, chestnut gelding hunter previously owned by Cosmo Gaffikin, purchased by George Sherston through Dixon and ridden during the narrator's formative hunting seasons

memes.label: Hoadley Rectory
memes.description: The rectory at Hoadley where Stephen Colwood lived as rector, a social center for young hunting men and site of Sherston's visits during the hunting season.

memes.label: Hoath Wood
memes.description: A sure-find covert in the Ringwell hunting country, approximately three miles from the narrator's starting point, known for producing good gallops

memes.label: Horsemanship
memes.description: The art and discipline of riding — the foundational skill of the foxhunting life that the narrator acquires under Dixon's tutelage

memes.label: Hunt Courtesy
memes.description: The unwritten code of conduct governing behaviour in the hunting field

memes.label: Hunting Season
memes.description: The annual cycle of the foxhunting calendar from autumn to spring

memes.label: Colonel Kinjack's Command
memes.description: Lieutenant-Colonel Kinjack takes command of the battalion on the Western Front, bringing rigorous military efficiency and a formidable reputation, Part 10.

memes.label: La Bassée Sector
memes.description: The narrator's first experience of the trenches in the La Bassée sector, including the Givenchy trenches, working parties at Festubert, and the battalion's withdrawal for Divisional Rest, from Part 10 of the memoir.

memes.label: Lord Dumborough
memes.description: Master of Foxhounds at Dumborough Park, the most terrifying figure in young George Sherston's early hunting world, whose formidable presence dominated the Dumborough Hunt country

memes.label: Mansfield
memes.description: A fellow officer during army training — competent and competent despite his New Army style

memes.label: Migration to the Midlands
memes.description: The narrator's decision to leave the Ringwell country and follow Denis Milden to hunt in the Midlands, marking a pivotal transition in Part 8 of the memoir.

memes.label: Military Training
memes.description: The infantry training experience that transforms civilians into soldiers in the pre-war army

memes.label: Miss Maskall
memes.description: Eccentric elderly lady of the neighbourhood, remembered for her habit of sitting on the top step of a ladder cutting her hedge, whose witty exchanges at the Flower Show Match delighted the young Sherston

memes.label: The Miss Pattons
memes.description: Two elderly sisters, regular attendees at local cricket matches and flower shows, who passed through the neighbourhood on their tricycles and won second prize for a tea-cosy at the Flower Show

memes.label: Mobilization
memes.description: The outbreak of the First World War in August 1914 and the enlistment of George Sherston and his hunting companions — the event that ended the Edwardian sporting world and sent the foxhunting men to the Western Front

memes.label: Morlancourt Interlude
memes.description: Peaceful Divisional Rest interlude at Morlancourt village with Dick Tiltwood and Mansfield, a respite from trench warfare during Part 10.

memes.label: Mr. Pennett
memes.description: The narrator's ex-guardian and acting trustee who opposed the purchase of Harkaway

memes.label: Mr. Star
memes.description: The narrator's gentle, semi-clerical retired schoolmaster who provided early education at home

memes.label: Mrs. Oakfield
memes.description: Mistress of Thurrow Park, a brilliant horsewoman and central figure of the Wednesday hunting country, admired for her style, generosity, and skill in managing Captain Harry Hinnycraft.

memes.label: Pacey's Plantation
memes.description: A hunt covert drawn by the Ringwell Hounds during Denis Milden's mastership, where a notable fox provided a short but eventful run.

memes.label: Packlestone
memes.description: Hunting country and village where Denis Milden became Master of Foxhounds, and where Dixon served as huntsman in the Packlestone Kennels.

memes.label: Park Wood
memes.description: A woodland covert in the Ringwell hunt country, drawn during the narrator's early hunting experiences.

memes.label: Point-to-Point Racing
memes.description: Amateur horse racing across country, a central sporting event of the Edwardian hunting calendar

memes.label: Potford Hounds
memes.description: Foxhunting pack based in the Potford country, central to Part 4 ("A Day with the Potford") of the memoir

memes.label: Potford Vale
memes.description: A hunting country vale where the narrator had a successful scramble across country, renewing his faith in foxhunting

memes.label: Pre-War Sporting Life
memes.description: The leisured sporting existence of the Edwardian gentry before the Great War

memes.label: "Prefatory Note"
memes.description: "I have been asked to write an explanatory preface to these Memoirs, on the occasion of their first appearance in a single volume. The first volume was begun ..."

memes.label: Rapworth Park
memes.description: The country estate of Sir John Ruddimore, a hunt magnate, where a lawn-meet was held during a period of transition for the Ringwell Hunt.

memes.label: Remount Service
memes.description: British Army Remount Service responsible for procuring and training horses for military use during the First World War

memes.label: Ringwell Country
memes.description: The hunting country on the far side of the county — home of the Ringwell Hunt, Denis Milden's mastership, and the landscape of the narrator's peak foxhunting years

memes.label: Rob Roy
memes.description: Rob Roy, a small black pony described as "a perfect picture of a miniature hunter," was George Sherston's first mount — the horse that taught him to ride and kindled his lifelong love of horses and foxhunting

memes.label: Rotherden
memes.description: Village in the Weald of Kent, home to the Rotherden cricket eleven and Rector of Rotherden, site of the Flower Show cricket match.

memes.label: Royal Field Artillery
memes.description: The Royal Field Artillery, the branch of the British Army in which Stephen Colwood served as a Special Reserve officer during the First World War

memes.label: Rural Church and Parish Life
memes.description: The role of the Church of England in Edwardian rural communities—churchwardens, parish clergy, Sunday services, and the social authority of the established church.

memes.label: Rural Estate Life
memes.description: The rhythms and routines of country house existence in pre-war England

memes.label: Sandhurst
memes.description: Royal Military College at Sandhurst, the British Army's officer training establishment, referenced in the memoir's military sections

memes.label: Sheila
memes.description: Sheila, a mouse-coloured Welsh cob pony, was George Sherston's second mount — the determined and sure-footed hunter that carried him through his debut with the Dumborough Hounds and his first real season of foxhunting

memes.label: Sherston
memes.description: The family surname of the narrator and his aunt, appearing throughout the memoir as George Sherston (the narrator/protagonist) and Miss Sherston (his aunt).

memes.label: Sir Jocelyn Porteus-Porteous
memes.description: Magnate of Folesford Hall and the predominant personage of the Saturday hunting country, a short, thickset man with a pompous manner punctuated by recurrent sniffs.

memes.label: Sir John Ruddimore
memes.description: Stolid and rather exclusive local magnate of Rapworth Park, one of the acknowledged "wiseacres" of the hunting field whose silent authority commanded respect among the sporting fraternity
aliases: Sir John Ruddimore of Rapworth Park

memes.label: Stephen Colwood
memes.description: The narrator's close friend and hunting companion, a fellow enthusiast of the sporting life

memes.label: Surrender Priestly's School
memes.description: The school briefly mentioned in the narrator's early life — part of the Edwardian educational landscape

memes.label: Church Bells
memes.description: The sound of church bells as a recurring sonic motif in the memoir, marking time, place, and emotional atmosphere from Butley village to the Western Front.

memes.label: The Cavalry
memes.description: The mounted military tradition and its relationship to the hunting field

memes.label: The Colonel's Cup
memes.description: The steeplechase for the Colonel's Cup, run as part of the Dumborough Hunt Steeplechase on Easter Monday, forms the narrative center of Part 6 of the memoir — a high-stakes race that tests the young narrator's nerve and skill.

memes.label: The Dumborough Hunt
memes.description: A local hunt in the Dumborough woodlands where the narrator experienced disappointing days before finding better sport elsewhere

memes.label: The Flower Show
memes.description: The annual village flower show — a quintessential Edwardian rural social gathering that framed the cricket match

memes.label: The Flower Show Match
memes.description: The annual village cricket match that was the sporting and social climax of Butley's summer — the occasion of the narrator's triumphant innings of sixty-four and a defining episode of Edwardian rural life

memes.label: The Front
memes.description: The Western Front in France and Flanders where Sherston served during the First World War, including the Givenchy trenches on the La Bassée Canal.

memes.label: The Hunt
memes.description: The central ritual of the foxhunting world — a day's hunting with hounds across the English countryside

memes.label: The Master of Hounds
memes.description: The role and authority of the Master of Foxhounds in the Edwardian hunting world

memes.label: The Ringwell Hunt
memes.description: The foxhunt that defined the narrator's peak sporting years — under Denis Milden's mastership, with its Point-to-Point races, supportive farmers, and the kennels that became a second home

memes.label: The Trenches
memes.description: The trench systems of the Western Front, specifically the Givenchy trenches on the La Bassée Canal, where Sherston experienced the realities of trench warfare in the First World War.

memes.label: The Weald
memes.description: The broad, hop-kiln-dotted landscape of Kent and Sussex forming the geographical backdrop of Sherston's early life, visible from the garden at Butley.

memes.label: The Yeomanry
memes.description: The mounted territorial cavalry regiment that became the natural destination for hunting men transitioning to military service — a bridge between the foxhunting world and the Western Front

memes.label: Thurrow Park
memes.description: The country estate of Mrs. Oakfield, a social hub for the fashionable Friday hunting set in the Saturday country near Folesford.

memes.label: Tom Dixon
memes.description: The groom who taught George Sherston to ride, fostered his love of foxhunting, and served as mentor, confidant, and friend from childhood through the narrator's early adulthood

memes.label: Tom Seamark
memes.description: The Rotherden cricket umpire, a red-faced sporting publican known for his stentorian decisions, jovial character, and emphatic umpiring mannerisms.

memes.label: Tony
memes.description: A young hunt member, referred to as "Tony" or "young Lewison," who sold his horse Cockbird to the narrator for fifty pounds.

memes.label: Trench Warfare
memes.description: The reality of the Western Front that eclipsed the Edwardian hunting world

memes.label: Yeomanry Training Camp
memes.description: Annual training camp for the Yeomanry territorial unit, part of the narrator's military service before deployment to the front.
